title: Community Page: Full Layout with Tabs, Posts, and Customization

requirements.text: Add backend data types and storage for community pages: CommunityPost (id, communityId, authorPrincipal, content, imageBlob, createdAt), CommunityTabOrder (communityId, tabOrder as array of tab names), CommunityTabPermissions (communityId, membersWithReorderPermission as array of principals), and extend the existing Community type to include bannerBlob (binary), bannerColor, bannerFont, accentColor. Provide canister methods: createPost(communityId, content, imageBlob?) -> PostId; getPosts(communityId) -> [CommunityPost]; updateBannerSettings(communityId, bannerBlob?, bannerColor?, bannerFont?, accentColor?) — host only; updateTabOrder(communityId, newOrder) — host or permitted member only; grantTabReorderPermission(communityId, principal) — host only; revokeTabReorderPermission(communityId, principal) — host only; getTabPermissions(communityId) -> [Principal]; getTabOrder(communityId) -> [Text].
acceptanceCriteria: createPost stores post on-chain and returns a unique PostId
getPosts returns all posts for a given communityId in reverse-chronological order
updateBannerSettings rejects callers who are not the community host
updateTabOrder accepts calls from the host or any principal in membersWithReorderPermission
grantTabReorderPermission and revokeTabReorderPermission are host-only
getTabOrder returns the persisted tab order, defaulting to [Home, Chat, Lore, Polls, Quizzes, Rules]
Banner image is stored as binary blob on-chain

requirements.text: The 'Open' button on the CommunityCard component (located at XPath /html[1]/body[1]/div[1]/div[1]/div[1]/div[1]/div[1]/div[1]/div[1]/section[1]/div[1]/div[1]/div[2]/button[1]) must navigate to a full CommunityPage view when clicked. This navigation must work and persist even after the user has left the Communities tab and returned — the community should remain accessible by storing the active community ID in component state or context, not just URL params.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking 'Open' on a community card renders the CommunityPage for that community
Navigating away from the Communities tab and returning still allows re-opening the community page
The CommunityPage is rendered within the Communities tab without affecting any other tab or route

requirements.text: Create a CommunityPage component rendered inside the Communities tab. It must include: (1) a full-width top banner section displaying the community's banner image (or a solid color fallback), community name, and a host-only 'Edit Banner' control; (2) a stats bar below the banner showing member count and total post count; (3) a horizontal tab bar (Home, Chat, Lore, Polls, Quizzes, Rules) rendered below the stats bar.
acceptanceCriteria: Banner image fills the full width of the community page header
If no banner image is set, the banner renders with the community's bannerColor as background
Stats bar displays current member count and post count fetched from the backend
Tab bar renders all six tabs: Home, Chat, Lore, Polls, Quizzes, Rules
Only the authenticated host sees the 'Edit Banner' control

requirements.text: Implement host-only banner customization from within the CommunityPage. The 'Edit Banner' control (host-only) opens an inline editor or modal allowing the host to: upload a new banner image (stored on-chain as binary), pick a banner background color, select a font for the community title, and pick an accent color. Changes are saved via the updateBannerSettings backend method and immediately reflected on the page.
acceptanceCriteria: Host can upload a banner image (PNG/JPG, max 5MB); it is stored on-chain and displayed immediately
Host can choose a background color used when no image is uploaded
Host can select a font for the community title from at least 4 preset options
Host can select an accent color applied to buttons and highlights in the community page
Non-host members do not see the 'Edit Banner' control
Changes persist across page reloads

requirements.text: Implement draggable/reorderable horizontal tabs on the CommunityPage. By default, only the community host can reorder tabs by dragging them. The host can toggle per-member tab-reorder permission (individually, per principal) from within the community page. Non-permitted members see the tabs as fixed and non-draggable. Tab order is persisted on-chain via updateTabOrder and loaded via getTabOrder on page mount.
acceptanceCriteria: Tabs are draggable and reorderable for the host
Tab order is saved to the backend after each reorder and restored on next page load
Host can grant or revoke tab-reorder permission to individual members via a permissions UI within the community page
Members without permission see static, non-draggable tabs
Members with granted permission can also drag and reorder tabs; their changes persist on-chain

requirements.text: Implement the Home tab content on the CommunityPage. It must include a post creation form (Internet Identity authentication required) with a text input and optional image upload (stored on-chain as binary via createPost). Below the form, render a chronological feed of all posts for the community, each showing author display name, post text, optional image, and timestamp. Fetched via getPosts.
acceptanceCriteria: Unauthenticated users cannot submit posts; a login prompt is shown instead
Authenticated users can type a post and optionally attach an image
Submitted posts appear in the feed immediately and persist on-chain
Post images are stored as binary on-chain and rendered inline in the feed
Each post displays the author's display name (or shortened principal), content, image (if any), and formatted timestamp

requirements.text: Implement the Chat tab content on the CommunityPage as a non-functional UI mockup placeholder. Render a greyed-out chat layout that visually resembles a messaging interface (input bar, message bubbles) but all interactive elements are disabled or visually muted. Display a 'Coming Soon' or 'Private messaging — coming soon' label overlay.
acceptanceCriteria: Chat tab renders a visually recognizable greyed-out chat UI
No interactive elements in the Chat tab are functional
A 'Coming Soon' or equivalent label is prominently displayed
The mockup does not make any backend calls

requirements.text: Implement placeholder layouts for the Lore, Polls, Quizzes, and Rules tabs on the CommunityPage. Each tab should render a styled placeholder card or section with the tab name as a heading and a 'Coming Soon' or informational message. These placeholders must visually match the community's theme (accent color, font).
acceptanceCriteria: Lore, Polls, Quizzes, and Rules tabs each render a distinct placeholder layout
Each placeholder displays the tab name as a heading and a descriptive 'Coming Soon' message
Placeholder styling uses the community's active accent color and font settings

requirements.text: Ensure communities remain accessible after the user navigates away from and returns to the Communities tab. The active community ID (if a community page was open) must be preserved in React state or context so the user is returned to the same community page view when they navigate back to the Communities tab.
acceptanceCriteria: Navigating to another tab and back to Communities preserves the open community page
If no community was open, the Communities hub list is shown as normal
No data or state outside the Communities tab is affected

requirements.text: Ensure all changes are strictly scoped to the Communities tab and its sub-components (CommunityCard, CommunitiesHubPage, and new CommunityPage). No files, components, hooks, or backend methods outside the Communities tab feature area should be modified.
acceptanceCriteria: SessionPage, LobbyPage, chat components, document components, and all non-community files remain unchanged
No new routes or navigation entries outside the Communities tab are added

constraints: Do not modify any files outside the Communities tab feature area (no changes to SessionPage, LobbyPage, chat, documents, or any non-community component)
Internet Identity authentication is required for creating communities and posts — no anonymous mode
All image uploads (banners, post images) must be stored on-chain as binary blobs in the Motoko backend
immutablePaths (frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, useActor.ts, main.tsx, frontend/src/components/ui) must not be modified
nonGoals: RPG-style features are explicitly out of scope
Real-time chat or functional private messaging (Chat tab is a placeholder only)
Polls, Quizzes, Lore, and Rules tab functionality (placeholders only)
Any changes to SessionPage, LobbyPage, or any component outside the Communities tab
External image hosting or URL-based image storage
